meaning: n.波尔卡舞,女用紧身短上衣
examples: The shoes have polka dots on them.
Think dark jeans with a bright polka-dot top, or contrast a nude shift dress with a tailored spotty jacket.
They also looked at whether clothing items had childlike characteristics, e.g. polka dot patterns and ribbons.
Clad in their polka-dot and plaid pajamas, the Parmas grabbed their dog, Boo, and fled.
In the mid-1960s, Mr. Henry wrote "Ballroom Polka, " which went on to become a standard.
